task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of OpenICE device simulators running for a Pulse Oximeter and a Noninvasive Blood Pressure (NIBP) monitor (either GUI windows or terminal output indicating they are active).
Visual evidence of OpenICE device simulators running for a Multiparameter Monitor, a Capnograph/CO2 monitor, and an Infusion Pump.

task_completion.description: Verify data flow in Vital Signs application
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of the OpenICE 'Vital Signs' clinical application open in the GUI, displaying synthetic patient data, numbers, or waveforms from the configured devices.
Visual evidence of a text editor or terminal creating/editing a file specifically named '/home/ga/Desktop/periop_handoff_checklist.txt'.

task_completion.description: Document devices, parameters, and summary note in the checklist
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of the checklist file's contents including the Pre-Op devices (Pulse Oximeter, NIBP), OR devices (Multiparameter, Capnograph, Infusion Pump), their monitored parameters, and a summary handoff note.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually launched the OpenICE simulators and Vital Signs app
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for actual GUI windows or active terminal processes for the OpenICE simulators and Vital Signs app. The agent should not just write the text file without running the software.

integrity.description: Ensure the checklist content is genuinely written and not just a copy-paste of the prompt
integrity.visual_evidence: The text file should contain specific monitored parameters (e.g., SpO2, HR, BP, CO2) and a coherent summary note, rather than just repeating the exact phrasing of the task description.

integrity.description: Ensure the Vital Signs app was viewed in the GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: The Vital Signs clinical application must be visible as a graphical window showing data flow, proving the agent visually verified the data as requested.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using the OpenICE platform to simulate a perioperative handoff. 5 simulated devices must be created: Pre-Op (Pulse Oximeter, NIBP) and Operating Room (Multiparameter Monitor, Capnograph, Infusion Pump). The 'Vital Signs' clinical application must be launched to verify data flow. Finally, a handoff checklist must be saved at /home/ga/Desktop/periop_handoff_checklist.txt containing the devices, monitored parameters, and a summary note. All data is synthetic.
